description: Hand-curated QA evaluation set for the AI-policy QA app. Ground truth was authored by directly reading the NCSL corpus (data/ncsl/us_ai_legislation_ncsl_text.jsonl). 20 questions span easy single-bill lookups through compound list+compare questions.

scoring_notes.required_keywords: Fraction of listed keywords (case-insensitive substring) found in answer text. 1.0 = all found.
scoring_notes.forbidden_keywords: If any listed keyword is found in answer, penalty applied.
scoring_notes.expected_bill_ids: Set-level precision/recall of cited bill_ids vs this set (normalized by collapsing whitespace).
scoring_notes.expected_count: Single integer expected. Answer graded by ratio to expected, clipped to [0, 1]. Tolerances allow partial credit.
scoring_notes.min_citations: Hard check on number of citation chunks returned.
scoring_notes.keyword_threshold: Minimum fraction of required_keywords that must be present for the question to be considered 'passed'.

questions.question: What is the title of Delaware House Bill 16 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Bill title is 'Artificial Intelligence Commission'. Trivial lookup.

questions.question: What is the status of Kentucky Senate Bill 4 from 2025?

ground_truth.notes: KY S 4 status is 'Enacted - Act No. 66'.

questions.question: In what year was Texas House Bill 149 (Regulation of the Use of Artificial Intelligence) enacted?

ground_truth.notes: TX H 149 was enacted in 2025.

questions.question: What topics does California Senate Bill 53 (Artificial Intelligence Models: Large Developers) address?

ground_truth.notes: Official topics: Cybersecurity, Government Use, Impact Assessment, Private Sector Use, Studies.

questions.question: Which California bill passed in 2025 is known as the California AI Transparency Act?

ground_truth.notes: Assembly Bill 853 (CA A 853), Enacted - Act No. 2025-674.

questions.question: Under Arizona Senate Bill 1295, what class of felony is criminal impersonation using computer-generated voice or video?

ground_truth.notes: Summary: 'criminal impersonation is a class 5 or 6 felony'. Accept either or both.

questions.question: What does Mississippi Senate Bill 2426 establish, and what is its charge?

ground_truth.notes: Establishes the Artificial Intelligence Regulation Task Force to study and evaluate AI applications, risks, and policy recommendations.

questions.question: What change does Delaware House Bill 16 make to the AI Commission's membership?

ground_truth.notes: Adds a high school student as a non-voting member of the AI Commission.

questions.question: Under New York Senate Bill 822, what must state agencies do regarding automated employment decision making tools?

ground_truth.notes: Requires state agencies to publish a list of automated employment decision making tools on the agency's website. Also maintains AI inventory.

questions.question: What does Montana Senate Bill 25 regulate?

ground_truth.notes: Regulates the use of deepfakes in election and electioneering communications.

questions.question: List the AI-related bills that Delaware enacted in 2025.

ground_truth.notes: Exactly two 2025 Delaware enacted bills: DE H 16 (AI Commission) and DE H 105 (Employment Practices).

questions.question: Which US states enacted AI-related bills that specifically address elections in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: 6 states, 7 bills. Corpus 'Elections' topic filtered to 2025 enacted.

questions.question: Approximately how many AI-related bills were enacted across US states and territories in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: Ground truth: 192 bills with status starting 'Enacted' in year 2025. Accept approx range 150-230.

questions.question: Which US state had the most AI-related bills enacted in 2025?

ground_truth.notes: California with 24 enacted 2025 AI bills.

questions.question: Name several 2025 enacted US state bills that specifically target deepfakes or synthetic media.

ground_truth.notes: At least 12 enacted 2025 bills with 'deepfake' or 'synthetic' in title. Precision/recall graded over the listed set; partial recall acceptable.

questions.question: What did California enact in 2025 concerning large AI model developers?

ground_truth.notes: CA SB 53, Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act.

questions.question: What are the bills that are about AI that are passed or enacted in 2025? What do they have in common?

ground_truth.notes: THE headline failing case. Ground truth: 192 enacted bills, recurring themes = deepfakes/synthetic media, election integrity, disclosure requirements, task forces/commissions, consumer protection, child safety. Evaluated on (1) count, (2) how many GT bills appear in citations, (3) coverage of common themes.

questions.question: Compare how Texas House Bill 149 and Colorado Senate Bill 4 (both 2025) approach AI regulation.

ground_truth.notes: TX HB 149: broader AI regulation with civil penalties. CO SB 4: specifically algorithmic systems transparency, effective by June 30, 2026.

questions.question: What common approach do Rhode Island HB 5872, Montana SB 25, and North Dakota HB 1167 share?

ground_truth.notes: All three regulate AI/deepfake/synthetic media in election communications. RI regulates synthetic media in elections; MT regulates deepfakes in election communications; ND requires AI disclosure statements for political ads.

questions.question: List 2025 enacted bills that address the use of AI in employment, and what do they require of employers or state agencies?

ground_truth.notes: NY SB 822 requires state agencies to publish lists of automated employment decision making tools; DE HB 105 requires salary range disclosures in job postings. Partial credit if either bill is cited.
